metadata.title: SDXL Refiner Ratio Comparison (60/40 vs 70/30 vs 80/20 vs 90/10 vs Base-only)
metadata.description: Compare different Base:Refiner step ratios to find optimal handoff point. Same prompt/seed, different split points.
metadata.author: comfyui-learning

experiment_design.objective: Find optimal Base:Refiner step split ratio

control_variables.prompt_positive: portrait of a young woman with freckles, natural lighting, shallow depth of field, 85mm lens, professional photography

expected_results.base_only: Good overall quality but may lack fine detail in skin/hair
expected_results.ratio_90_10: Nearly identical to base-only with subtle texture improvement
expected_results.ratio_80_20: Best balance - composition from base preserved, details enhanced
expected_results.ratio_70_30: More polished but refiner style may start overriding base's character
expected_results.ratio_60_40: Risk of composition drift - refiner has too much influence on structure

what_to_look_for: Skin texture and pore detail (refiner should improve this)
Background detail consistency (refiner may change background)
Overall color palette shift (refiner tends toward more neutral tones)
Composition preservation (too much refiner = layout changes)

nodes_template._note: Conceptual template for each variant. In practice, create 5 parallel pipelines sharing the same checkpoint loaders and latent.
